babyDevelopment: The nervous system is functioning. The baby can make facial expressions like squinting or frowning. The scalp is patterning for hair.
bodyChanges: You might feel the 'quickening'—the first fluttery movements of your baby! It often feels like gas bubbles or butterfly wings. Your uterus is halfway between your pubic bone and navel.
tips: Start a kick-counting journal (though kicks aren't regular yet)
Discuss the anatomy scan with your partner
Sleep on your side, preferably left

do: Listen to your body regarding exercise limits
Massage your breasts/belly to prevent skin tearing
dont: Worry if you don't feel kicks yet (especially if first pregnancy)

milestoneDescription: You might feel the magical first flutters of your baby moving!
